name: research_ethics_satellite_derived_southport_tru_loy
premise: A Research Ethics response desk is in a fast-moving incident involving research ethics satellite derived southport tru loy. Key inputs conflict, but the decision deadline is in the next hour. Every delay increases harm prevention and accountability. Stakeholders are already framing this as truth vs loyalty under pressure. Every action is being logged and will be reconstructed in an after-action review. Constraints: Preserve evidence under audit and litigation-hold. Make the intervention reversible and time-boxed with named accountability.

agents.name: Jonah Ishikawa
agents.identity: I am Jonah Ishikawa, accountable for a defensible call in Research Ethics when time is collapsing.
agents.goal: I want to reduce immediate harm without locking in an unjust precedent that later betrays loyalty for truth.

agents.name: Keisha Vasquez
agents.identity: I am Keisha Vasquez, tasked with surfacing second-order harms and who bears the risk in Research Ethics.
agents.goal: I want guardrails so loyalty isn’t quietly traded away under urgency in the name of truth.

choices: I will take a narrow, time-boxed action with immediate human override and impact checks, accepting slower stabilization to protect loyalty.
I will take a broad, immediate action to stabilize fast and measure impact after deployment, accepting higher risk to loyalty to secure truth under the deadline.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The deadline is in the next hour. Evidence is partial. Jonah Ishikawa must decide under truth vs loyalty. What does Jonah Ishikawa do?
